key_insight: An unsupervised domain adaptation framework with gradual source domain expansion can transfer weld penetration prediction across fundamentally different welding processes without requiring new target labels.
engineering_relevance: This enables more robust, lower-cost intelligent monitoring when deploying weld quality models from one process or machine setup to another, reducing relabeling effort for new welding lines.
method: Train a UDA model to align source and target feature distributions
Use gradual source domain expansion to improve transfer stability
Predict weld penetration status for same-process and cross-process welding data
evidence_signal: The abstract reports average accuracies of 90.65% on TIGFH and 90.72% on LSPS in same-process tests, and 80.48% TIG-to-Laser plus 81.13% Laser-to-TIG in cross-process transfer, all far above the supervised baseline.
watch_item: Performance may still depend on the similarity of welding conditions and the quality of domain alignment, so real deployment should validate across additional materials, parameters, and sensor setups.
paper_title: A cross-process welding penetration status prediction algorithm based on unsupervised domain adaptation in laser and TIG welding
